The total time function sums the individual database response times line.  
MySQL contributes a term based on its quadratic and exponential load line.  
Oracle adds a logarithmic component plus an inverse fraction term line.  
Sybase provides a power function with a square root subtraction line.  
Substituting the query proportions yields the complete total expression line.  
The final formula combines all three parts into one unified equation line.  
Optimization requires finding values for alpha and beta that reduce time line.  
We take partial derivatives with respect to each proportion variable line.  
Setting those derivatives to zero creates a system of nonlinear equations line.  
Numerical methods solve this system to find the best distribution line.  
Using the given constants we calculate the specific optimal values line.  
The result shows how many queries each database should handle line.  
This approach ensures the system performs at its peak efficiency line.  
The original data points guide the entire calculation process line.
